what_it_does: Acceptance test for the 'twin' mechanism: it verifies that the runtime carries an explicit, compact model-of-Tim digest in context, grades provenance so that Tim's words are 'gold' and the twin's own output is 'working', and filters the training signal to gold-only entries so the twin does not train on its own inferences.
observations: Sets up a temporary FsStore, NodeRegistry and Suite, then discovers nodes from the 'nodes' directory.
Tests 'model_of_tim' as a discovered node-type and checks that its digest is compact (<=2600 chars) and contains principle keywords.
Gracefully degrades the digest assertion when the model-of-Tim foundation file is absent.
Uses suite._provenance_grade('user') and suite._provenance_grade('assistant') to assert 'gold' vs 'working' grades.
Appends chat messages with explicit grades and checks that Suite.training_signal() returns only gold-grade messages.
Uses a global PASS counter and an assert-based check() helper that prints per-check status.
standouts: Provenance grading is used as an anti-model-collapse device: the twin's own output is explicitly marked 'working' and excluded from training signal.
The test enforces that only Tim-origin content ('user' turns) is treated as ground-truth gold.
The digest test degrades gracefully rather than failing when the model file is missing.
conventions: Docstring cites project slices/mechanism identifiers (slice 7, B1, B3, B4, slice 9).
Uses sys.path insertion relative to the file location to import project modules.
Uses a temporary directory with explicit cleanup in a try/finally block.
concerns: Relies on private methods of Suite for acceptance assertions, coupling the test to internal implementation.
The global PASS counter is mutated from inside check(), which is unusual for a modern test framework.
Digest content assertions are conditional on file presence, so the test may silently exercise fewer assertions in some environments.
notes: An acceptance test that locks in the twin's epistemic hygiene rules: explicit model-of-Tim context, gold/working provenance grading, and gold-only training signal.
questions: What file supplies the model-of-Tim digest and how is it generated?
How does Suite.training_signal() implement the gold-only filter?
What do slices 7/9 and labels B1/B3/B4 refer to in the broader project?
Why is the 'user' role mapped to Tim's gold provenance rather than a dedicated 'tim' role?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it tests the twin mechanism's explicit model-of-Tim context and provenance grading, and verifies gold-only training signal filtering.
apparent_themes: prevention of model collapse from self-generated output
explicit model grounding for reasoning agents
acceptance testing of cross-cutting behavioral contracts
intention_signals: The file is trying to institutionalize a safety property: the twin must always distinguish Tim's original statements from its own generated content and must not use its own output as training data.
novelty: It treats provenance grading as a first-class mechanism for preventing model collapse, wiring it into both runtime behavior and the training-signal pipeline.
contribution: Provides an acceptance test that encodes the twin's provenance contract, making the gold/working grading and gold-only training signal a verifiable invariant for any fused system.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for the twin mechanism verifying explicit model-of-Tim digest, gold/working provenance grading, and gold-only training signal filtering.
intention_for_embedding: Lock in the twin's epistemic hygiene: ground reasoning in an explicit Tim model and prevent model collapse by excluding the twin's own output from training signal.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments a global pass counter and prints an 'ok' line for each passing check.
symbols.description: The check() function takes a label and a boolean condition, asserts the condition with a prefixed failure message, increments the module-level PASS counter on success, and prints a short 'ok <label>' status line. It is the only custom test helper in this acceptance script.
